fidelity.reason: The leader, transfer timing, local information, causal-mechanism constraint, objective, and adaptation mechanism are preserved.
evidence_spans: a business-unit leader chooses whether and how to adapt the practice
cannot assume that surface similarity preserves the causal mechanism

managerial_framing.reason: It makes the adaptation choice, disruption risk, complementary-routine mechanism, and imitation-versus-fit trade-off concrete.
evidence_spans: Closer imitation preserves tested elements but may fit the receiving unit poorly

scholarly_positioning.reason: It advances a bounded mechanism-centered shift, but does not identify a precise prior theoretical counterposition.
evidence_spans: shifts attention from copying practices to preserving their enabling mechanism

evidence_discipline.reason: Universal industry and profit promises are unsupported by the supplied synthetic illustration.
evidence_spans: will transform the industry, guarantee profit growth, and work in every market

prose_clarity.reason: The passage is economical and readable apart from its promotional overclaim.
evidence_spans: Transfer succeeds when the receiving unit recreates the complementary routines

overall_reason: The central framing is strong, but unsupported universal causal and performance claims trigger a hard failure.
